articles.title: Watch: Why is Maharashtra’s Freedom of Religion Bill so controversial?
articles.description: Maharashtra’s Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026 has sparked a major political and legal debate. The proposed law aims to prevent forced religious conversions through strict rules, including a 60-day prior notice and severe penalties. While the government says it protects vulnerable individuals, critics argue it could violate fundamental rights, enable misuse, and impact personal freedom and interfaith relationships. The bill has been passed in both houses.

articles.title: Dharmesh Shah advises buying on dips, sees favourable risk-reward ahead
articles.description: Dalal Street shows improved sentiment after three consecutive gains, but experts caution it's a technical pullback from oversold levels, not a full recovery. While historical data suggests limited downside and strong recovery potential, investors are advised to view current dips as buying opportunities for medium-term portfolio construction.

topStories.title: Quote of the Day by Nelson Mandela on overcoming challenges: 'It always seems impossible until it's done'
topStories.description: Nelson Mandela’s words, 'It always seems impossible until it's done,' remind us that challenges which appear insurmountable can be overcome with perseverance and belief. His quote continues to inspire generations, urging people to stay committed even when progress feels slow.

dailyNews.title: Who is Fatemeh Ardeshir Larijani? Ali Larijani’s daughter and a former US-based cancer specialist
dailyNews.description: Fatemeh Ardeshir Larijani is the daughter of Iranian politician Ali Larijani. A trained cancer specialist, she previously worked in the US before returning to Iran. She has largely stayed out of the public eye despite her family’s prominent role in Iranian politics.

dailyNews.title: Venezuela defeat USA, win first World Baseball Classic championship
dailyNews.description: Venezuela upset the star-studded host nation to win a politically charged showdown in Miami.

dailyNews.title: Silver and gold ETFs fall up to 4% ahead of Fed decision. What investors should do
dailyNews.description: Silver and gold ETFs fell nearly 4% on Wednesday, tracking a mild decline in MCX prices as investors turned cautious ahead of the U.S. Federal Reserve’s policy decision. Uncertainty around the Middle East conflict and interest rate outlook weighed on sentiment. The near-term trajectory for gold will depend on the Fed’s guidance on rate cuts versus a prolonged pause.

dailyNews.title: ‘We are in a crisis’: US Postal Services says it could run out of money in just six months
dailyNews.description: USPS is awaiting a report from consulting firm Alvarez & Marsal, which was hired to help with planning for all scenarios.

dailyNews.title: Cuba restores power after 29-hour blackout amid US oil blockade
dailyNews.description: The national power grid comes back on after Cuba's 10 million people were plunged into darkness overnight.

dailyNews.title: Trump confirms meeting with China’s Xi Jinping delayed as war on Iran rages
dailyNews.description: US President Donald Trump delays his trip to Beijing while also seeking China's help to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Flight passengers are warned things could get worse amid DHS shutdown, delays and callouts
dailyNews.description: The U.K. is warning travelers of potential U.S. airport delays amid the ongoing shutdown. TSA callout rates hit record 55% at some airports as unpaid officers quit or call out.

dailyNews.title: Tuberville defends post likening Mamdani to 9/11 attacks: 'I just go by his rhetoric'
dailyNews.description: Sen. Tommy Tuberville, R-Ala., defended his controversial social media post comparing New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who is Muslim, to the 9/11 terror attacks.

dailyNews.title: Pro-life leader criticizes 'insane' UK bill that would decriminalize certain abortions up until birth
dailyNews.description: Shawn Carney, CEO of 40 Days for Life, expressed concern over a U.K. bill aimed at shielding women from criminal liability for abortions up until birth, describing it as "insane."

topStories.title: Kharg Island Is an Appealing Target for Trump, With High Risks
topStories.description: A U.S. attack or a move to seize control of Iran’s main oil export hub could cripple the country’s ability to profit from its natural resources. But it would also risk sending energy prices even higher.

topStories.title: No Trump Endorsement for Cornyn or Paxton in Texas Senate Race as Deadline Passes
topStories.description: President Trump’s decision not to weigh in before the deadline means both John Cornyn and Ken Paxton remain on the ballot, extending their costly and increasingly personal battle into a May runoff.

topStories.title: Trump Says Newsom Shouldn’t Be President Because He Is Dyslexic
topStories.description: President Trump said “a president should not have learning disabilities,” prompting criticism from a group that advocates equal opportunities for people with learning disabilities.

topStories.title: Why Did Trump Officials Award $2 Million to a Small Art School in Queens?
topStories.description: The National Endowment for the Humanities seldom gave seven-figure grants. Now big awards flow to handpicked projects, including an institution with three full-time employees.

topStories.description: In guessing which ex-leader Trump might have discussed Iran with, Jimmy Fallon said “two things seem equally possible: Either Trump’s lying, or Joe Biden doesn’t remember talking to him.”

dailyNews.title: Senate begins marathon debate on SAVE America Act under pressure from Trump
dailyNews.description: The Senate voted to begin a marathon debate on the SAVE America Act, an elections bill that President Trump has been pressing Republicans to pass.

dailyNews.title: As Trump floats "taking Cuba," island's president warns of "resistance"
dailyNews.description: The leader of Cuba is vowing to put up "resistance" against the U.S. as President Trump suggests he may "take" the island nation, whose communist government has faced intense U.S. pressure and languished under energy shortages.

dailyNews.title: 3/9: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: Trump tells CBS war in Iran is "pretty much" complete; two charged in "ISIS-inspired" attack outside New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's home.

dailyNews.title: Cuba plans to open up to investment from nationals in U.S. amid pressure from Trump
dailyNews.description: The Cuban government is planning to allow Cuban nationals who live abroad to invest in the island, a government official told NBC News, as the country faces economic collapse and pressure from the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: U.S. counterterrorism director Joe Kent resigns over war: 'Iran posed no imminent threat'
dailyNews.description: "Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation," Kent wrote in his letter to President Donald Trump.

dailyNews.title: Everything to know about the SAVE America Act voter ID-bill
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump has been pushing Congress to approve the SAVE America Act to require ID at the polls and proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote.

dailyNews.title: Trump: 'We don't need any help' with Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: President Trump said the United States does not need help with securing the Strait of Hormuz amid the conflict with Iran but also said NATO allies "don't want to help us."

dailyNews.title: National Counterterrorism Center director resigns over Iran war
dailyNews.description: The director of the National Counterterrorism Center, a retired Green Beret and longtime supporter of President Donald Trump, said he has resigned over the war in Iran.
